evidence: File header cites 'Magic Square of Squares - Wikipedia' and the decl asks whether a 3x3 magic square with nine distinct nonzero square entries exists.
verify_note: Decl exists at MagicSquares.exists_magic_square_squares and Q135299535 is specifically the unsolved 3x3 magic-square-of-squares problem, exactly the existence question the decl states.

evidence: The statement requires all rows, columns, and diagonals of the 3x3 matrix to sum to the same value, i.e. a magic square.
verify_note: The statement requires equal sums over all rows, columns, and both diagonals — the magic-square condition is the core structure.

evidence: Every entry of the sought matrix must be a distinct positive square (IsSquare (m i j)).
verify_note: Every entry must satisfy 0 < m i j and IsSquare (m i j), so square numbers are load-bearing.

evidence: The docstring asks for a 3x3 semi-magic square (all rows and columns sum to the same total) with distinct positive integer cube entries.
verify_note: Decl exists and the semi-magic square (equal row and column sums) with cube entries is the statement's core structure; magic square is the host concept.

evidence: File is titled 'Mahler's 3/2 Problem' referencing that Wikipedia page, and the decl states the Mahler Conjecture that no non-zero Z-numbers exist.
verify_note: Decl exists and states exactly that no non-zero Z-numbers exist, which is Mahler's 3/2 problem; QID label verified.

evidence: Solved Flatto-Lagarias-Pollington bound Omega(p/q) > 1/p is stated as partial progress on Mahler's 3/2 problem in the same file.
verify_note: Decl exists (Omega(p/q) > 1/p solved bound) and is the file's stated partial progress on Mahler's 3/2 problem — genuine mentions.

evidence: The MLC conjecture states that the Mandelbrot set is locally connected, per the header reference to Mandelbrot_set#Local_connectivity.
verify_note: MLC : LocallyConnectedSpace mandelbrotSet — the Mandelbrot set is the subject of the statement.

evidence: The conjectured property is LocallyConnectedSpace mandelbrotSet — local connectivity is the load-bearing concept.
verify_note: LocallyConnectedSpace is the conjectured property itself; QID label 'locally connected space' verified.

evidence: The stronger MLC version states that all Multibrot sets are locally connected.
verify_note: Decl states LocallyConnectedSpace (multibrotSet n) for all n — Multibrot set is load-bearing; QID label verified.

evidence: The density of hyperbolicity conjecture states that parameters with attracting cycles are dense in the Mandelbrot set.
verify_note: Decl states density of attracting-cycle parameters in mandelbrotSet as claimed.

evidence: The density of hyperbolicity conjecture stated for multibrotSet n with 2 <= n.
verify_note: Decl states the density conjecture for multibrotSet n with hn : 2 <= n as claimed.

evidence: The boundary of the Mandelbrot set is conjectured to have zero area (volume (frontier mandelbrotSet) = 0).
verify_note: Decl states volume (frontier mandelbrotSet) = 0 as claimed.

evidence: The boundary of any Multibrot set is conjectured to have zero area.
verify_note: Decl states volume (frontier (multibrotSet n)) = 0 for any n as claimed.

evidence: File header cites the Wikipedia 'Mean value problem' page and the decl states Smale's conjecture |p(z)-p(c)|/|z-c| <= |p'(z)| for some critical point c (the K=1 case).
verify_note: Decl exists and states the K=1 Smale inequality, which is the mean value problem itself (Q28126647 desc confirms); exact formalizes despite an unused K binder.

evidence: Smale's proven K=4 weaker version of the mean value problem.
verify_note: Decl exists with the 4 * |p'(z)| bound — Smale's proven weaker version, correctly graded mentions.

evidence: Tischler's (d-1)/d bound for the mean value problem when p has only real roots.
verify_note: Decl exists with the (d-1)/d bound under all-real-roots hypothesis (Tischler), correctly graded mentions.

evidence: Tischler's (d-1)/d bound for the mean value problem when all roots of p have the same norm.
verify_note: Decl exists with the (d-1)/d bound under equal-norm-roots hypothesis (Tischler), correctly graded mentions.

evidence: File header cites 'Wikipedia: Mersenne conjectures' and the decl states the New Mersenne (Bateman-Selfridge-Wagstaff) conjecture, the main subject of that item.
verify_note: Wrong-grade formalizes: Q2739186 'Mersenne conjectures' is the group item (original Mersenne conjecture + New Mersenne conjecture + Lenstra-Pomerance-Wagstaff), while the decl formalizes only the New Mersenne conjecture — a sub-case; should be mentions.

evidence: Condition 1 of the conjecture is that 2^p - 1 (the Mersenne number) is prime.
verify_note: Condition 1 of NewMersenneConjectureStatement is (mersenne p).Prime — Mersenne prime is load-bearing.

evidence: Condition 2 of the conjecture is that (2^p + 1)/3 is prime, i.e. p gives a Wagstaff prime.
verify_note: Condition 2 is GivesWagstaffPrime, i.e. (2^p+1)/3 prime; QID label 'Wagstaff prime' verified.

evidence: Variant restricting the New Mersenne Conjecture statement to odd primes p.
verify_note: Decl new_mersenne_conjecture.variants.prime exists and restricts the New Mersenne Conjecture to odd primes — genuine mentions of the Mersenne conjectures topic.

evidence: The decl asks whether the set of p with (mersenne p).Prime is infinite — the open infinitude question for Mersenne primes.
verify_note: Decl asks whether {p | (mersenne p).Prime} is infinite — the Mersenne-prime infinitude question.

evidence: Catalan's Mersenne conjecture about the recursively defined Catalan-Mersenne numbers c_{n+1} = 2^{c_n} - 1; the referenced Wikipedia page redirects to Double Mersenne number#Catalan-Mersenne number conjecture.
verify_note: Decl exists and 'Catalan's Mersenne conjecture' verified to redirect to Double Mersenne number#Catalan-Mersenne number conjecture; QID label verified.

evidence: Mills' theorem (1947): there is a real A > 1 with floor(A^{3^n}) always prime; the least such A is Mills' constant, per the header reference.
verify_note: Decl exists' is Mills' theorem, the existence statement underlying Mills' constant (the least such A), the file's sole topic — genuine mentions.

evidence: The decl asserts there is a least Mills number, which is the definition of Mills' constant.
verify_note: IsMinMills A (the least Mills number) is by definition Mills' constant; decl exists.

evidence: Saito (2024): Mills' constant is irrational, cited in the header references.
verify_note: Decl states Irrational A for IsMinMills A (Saito 2024) as claimed.

evidence: Caldwell-Cheng (2005): assuming RH, Mills' constant begins 1.3063778838...
verify_note: Decl places the minimal Mills number in Ioo 1.3063778838 1.3063778839 under RH (Caldwell-Cheng) as claimed.

evidence: The bound is stated under the hypothesis hRH : RiemannHypothesis.
verify_note: hRH : RiemannHypothesis is an explicit hypothesis of the statement — load-bearing.

evidence: The decl states the Modularity Theorem (formerly Shimura-Taniyama-Weil conjecture) that every elliptic curve over Q is modular, citing the Wikipedia Modularity theorem page.
verify_note: Decl asserts modularityConjecture E (existence of a weight-2 normalised eigenform with a_p f = E.ap p) for every rational elliptic curve — the a_p form of the modularity theorem itself; QID label verified.

evidence: The statement quantifies over rational elliptic curves (WeierstrassCurve Q with IsElliptic).
verify_note: The statement quantifies over E : WeierstrassCurve Q with [E.IsElliptic] — elliptic curves are load-bearing.

evidence: The a_p version associates to each curve a normalised eigenform (CuspForm (Gamma0 N) 2) whose Fourier coefficients match E's point counts.
verify_note: The witness is a CuspForm (Gamma0 N) 2 normalised eigenform — the modular form is load-bearing.

evidence: File is titled 'Moser's Worm' referencing that Wikipedia page, and the decl asks for the greatest lower bound on the area of a shape covering every unit-length curve.
verify_note: Decl exists and asks for the GLB of areas of sets covering every unit worm under rigid motions — Moser's worm problem itself; QID label verified.

evidence: Norwood-Poole (2003) upper bound: a worm cover of area 0.260437 exists.
verify_note: Decl states existence of a worm cover of area 0.260437 (Norwood-Poole) as claimed.

evidence: The convex variant of Moser's worm problem asks for the minimal area of a convex worm cover.
verify_note: Decl states the convex-variant GLB question of the same problem as claimed.

evidence: The cover is required to satisfy Convex R X.
verify_note: The cover must satisfy Convex R X — convexity is load-bearing; QID label verified.

evidence: The minimal area in the convex worm problem is attained, via the Blaschke selection theorem.
verify_note: Decl states IsLeast for the convex worm-cover areas (attainment via Blaschke selection) as claimed.

evidence: Wang (2006): a convex set of area 0.270911861 covers all worms.
verify_note: Decl states a convex cover of area 0.270911861 exists (Wang 2006) as claimed.

evidence: Khandhawit-Pagonakis-Sriswasdi (2013): 0.232239 is a lower bound for the convex worm cover area.
verify_note: Decl states 0.232239 is in lowerBounds of convex worm-cover areas (Khandhawit-Pagonakis-Sriswasdi) as claimed.
